interactions.summary: Hasan asks where Jiang places himself politically; Jiang says he has always been on the left in commitments to redistribution, free speech, antiwar politics, anti-empire, and individual autonomy, though those preferences now make many people label him far right.

Hasan asks for Jiang's big 2026 prediction; Jiang gives three war predictions: U.S. ground troops will enter and become a quagmire requiring a draft, Israel and the United States will not use nuclear weapons, and somehow the Al-Aqsa Mosque will be destroyed. Hasan registers dread and closes.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Hasan final question and Jiang answer.

speaker_notes.note: The short 'I agree with that / I'm with you / Okay' interjections inside seg-0076 appear to be Hasan overlapping or transcript attribution bleed inside Jiang's answer.

claims.claim: Jiang locates his own politics in left commitments: wealth redistribution, free speech, antiwar politics, anti-empire, and individual autonomy.
Jiang says people would now likely consider him far right despite his left self-description.
Jiang predicts the United States will use ground troops in the Iran war, those troops will become a quagmire, and the United States will have to call up a national draft.
Jiang predicts Israel and the United States will not use nuclear weapons in the war and calls nuclear talk fearmongering.
Jiang predicts that during the course of the war the Al-Aqsa Mosque will somehow be destroyed.
claims.temporal_scope: Most controversial 2026 war prediction given on 2026-04-03.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang describes a political inversion: the positions he reads as left now get him classified as far right.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the interview's ideological conflict a self-description rather than leaving it only in Hasan's accusations.

signature_moments.moment: The final prediction turns from military sequence to sacred catastrophe.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the Al-Aqsa Mosque will be destroyed
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the interview's closing dark charge and the public read should preserve its shock and uncertainty.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's predicted consequence of U.S. ground troops becoming trapped in an Iran-war quagmire.
The Jerusalem holy site Jiang predicts will be destroyed during the war, without specifying mechanism.

chronology_notes.note: At the end of the 2026-04-03 interview Jiang gives his testable 2026 prediction set: U.S. ground troops and draft, no nuclear use, and destruction of Al-Aqsa.

uncertainty_notes.note: Jiang gives no mechanism for the Al-Aqsa prediction in this focus ref; preserve the uncertainty rather than filling it in.
